name: Jatamansi Churna

name: Tagar Churna

name: Pippali Churna
dosage: 0.5–2g with honey or ghee twice daily
drug_interactions: Cyclosporine/Tacrolimus — piperine inhibits CYP3A4; may increase drug levels significantly
Statins (simvastatin, atorvastatin) — piperine increases bioavailability; dose adjustment may be needed
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana 25/52; Ashtanga Hridayam Su.6

name: Shunti Churna
ingredients: Shunti / Dry Ginger (Zingiber officinale) — dried rhizome powder

drug_interactions: Warfarin — additive antiplatelet effect at high doses; monitor
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana 25; Ashtanga Hridayam Su.6/8

name: Maricha Churna
dosage: 0.25–1g with honey or warm water twice daily
drug_interactions: Cyclosporine/Tacrolimus — piperine (bioenhancer) inhibits CYP3A4; increases drug levels
Phenobarbital — piperine may alter drug levels; monitor
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana 25; Ashtanga Hridayam Su.6

name: Bala Churna
dosage: 3–6g with warm milk or ghee twice daily
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Sutra Sthana 15/11 (Vata shamana dravyas); CS Chi.28

name: Eranda Mula Churna
dosage: 3–5g with warm water or castor oil at bedtime
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Kalpa Sthana 4 (Eranda Kalpa); Ashtanga Hridayam Chi.21

name: Kumari Churna
ingredients: Kumari / Aloe vera (Aloe barbadensis) — dried leaf pulp powder
dosage: 1–3g with warm water or rose water twice daily
drug_interactions: Antidiabetics — additive glucose-lowering; monitor blood sugar
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita Chi.20 (Rasayana); Bhavaprakasha Nighantu — Guduchyadi varga

name: Karela Churna
dosage: 1–3g with warm water before meals twice daily
drug_interactions: Antidiabetics (Metformin, insulin, sulfonylureas) — SIGNIFICANT potentiation; monitor blood glucose closely and reduce doses as needed
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 12/15 (Prameha Chikitsa)

name: Methi Churna
dosage: 3–6g with warm water or buttermilk before meals
drug_interactions: Antidiabetics — additive hypoglycemic effect; monitor blood glucose
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 12/10 (Prameha Chikitsa)

name: Haridra Churna
dosage: 1–3g with warm milk (Haldi doodh) or warm water twice daily
anupana: Warm milk with black pepper or warm water

anupana_by_condition.arthritis: warm milk with black pepper (piperine enhances absorption)

drug_interactions: Warfarin/anticoagulants — additive antiplatelet effect at therapeutic doses; monitor INR
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana 4/18; Ashtanga Hridayam Chi.19/11

name: Ela Churna
ingredients: Ela / Green Cardamom (Elettaria cardamomum) — seed powder
dosage: 0.5–1.5g with warm water or honey twice daily
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana 25/49; Ashtanga Hridayam Su.6

name: Twak Churna
ingredients: Twak / True Cinnamon (Cinnamomum zeylanicum) — bark powder
dosage: 1–3g with warm water or honey twice daily
drug_interactions: Warfarin — coumarins in cinnamon have mild anticoagulant effect at high doses; monitor INR
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana 25; Ashtanga Hridayam Su.15

name: Chirayata Churna
dosage: 1–3g with warm water or honey twice daily
drug_interactions: Antidiabetics — potentiates glucose-lowering effect; monitor blood sugar
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 1/149 (Jwara); Charaka Samhita Chi.3

name: Kutaj Churna
dosage: 3–6g with buttermilk or warm water twice daily
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana 19 (Atisara); Ashtanga Hridayam Chi.10

name: Shatapushpa Churna
dosage: 1–3g with warm water or buttermilk twice daily
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 1; Bhavaprakasha Nighantu — Haritakyadi varga

name: Giloy Satva
ingredients: Guduchi Satva (Tinospora cordifolia) — aqueous extract starch
dosage: 0.5–1g with honey or warm water twice daily
anupana: Honey, warm water, or warm milk
drug_interactions: Immunosuppressants — may reduce drug effect due to immunostimulatory action
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana 25/42 (Jwaraghna); CS Chi.1 (Rasayana)
classical_action: Rasayana, Jwaraghna, Pramehaghna, Medhya, Tridosha hara

name: Kesar (Saffron) Milk Powder
ingredients: Kesar (Crocus sativus) — dried stigma powder prepared with milk base
dosage: 125–250mg (of pure kesar) with warm milk at bedtime
drug_interactions: Antidepressants (SSRIs) — possible serotonin-related interaction at high doses; consult physician
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Kalpa Sthana 1 (Kumkuma); Ashtanga Hridayam Su.15

name: Lavangadi Churna
dosage: 0.5–1.5g with honey or warm water after meals
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 10 (Atisara); Sharangdhara Samhita

name: Vaishwanara Churna
dosage: 3–6g with warm water or buttermilk before meals
drug_interactions: Antihypertensives — sodium content (lavana) may reduce efficacy
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 10; Sharangdhara Samhita Madhyama Khanda

name: Indukantham Kashayam
dosage: 15–20ml twice daily with equal water, warm, on empty stomach
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa Sthana 21 (Vatavyadhi); Kerala classical formulary

name: Amruthotharam Kashayam
dosage: 15–20ml twice daily with warm water, on empty stomach
drug_interactions: Antidiabetics — additive effect; monitor blood glucose
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa Sthana 1 (Jwara); Kerala classical formulary

name: Drakshadi Kashayam
dosage: 15–20ml twice daily with warm water
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 3 (Raktapitta); Kerala classical formulary

name: Sukumar Kashayam
dosage: 15–20ml twice daily on empty stomach
drug_interactions: Oral contraceptives — potential interaction with hormonal balance; consult physician
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 1 (Prasava Krichra); Kerala classical formulary

name: Pathyadi Kashayam
dosage: 15–20ml twice daily with warm water
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Uttara Sthana 13 (Shiro Roga); Charaka Samhita Chi.26

name: Balarishta
dosage: 15–30ml with equal water after meals
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa Sthana 21 (Vatavyadhi); Charaka Samhita Chi.28

name: Chandanasava
dosage: 15–30ml with equal water after meals twice daily
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 11 (Mutraghata); Charaka Samhita Chi.26

name: Ushirasava
dosage: 15–30ml with equal water after meals
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 3 (Raktapitta); Charaka Samhita Chi.4

name: Parthadyarishta
dosage: 15–30ml with equal water after meals twice daily
drug_interactions: Cardiac glycosides (Digoxin) — Arjuna has cardiac glycoside-like activity; monitor carefully
Antihypertensives — additive hypotensive effect; monitor BP
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 5 (Hridroga); Charaka Samhita Chi.26

name: Mridvikasava
dosage: 15–30ml with equal water after meals
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa 17 (Kasa); Ashtanga Hridayam Chi.3

name: Saptavinshati Guggulu
ingredients: Guggulu (Commiphora mukul) — 27-ingredient classical formula including Triphala, Trikatu, Chitrak, and other rasayanas
dosage: 1–2 tablets (500mg each) twice daily with warm water
drug_interactions: Thyroid medications — Guggulu may affect T3/T4 levels; monitor
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 21 (Vatavyadhi); Sharangdhara Samhita

name: Navaka Guggulu
dosage: 1–2 tablets (500mg) twice daily with warm water
drug_interactions: Thyroid medications — Guggulu may alter thyroid hormone levels; monitor T3/T4
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 14 (Sthoulya — Medoroga); Sharangdhara

name: Medohar Guggulu
dosage: 1–2 tablets (500mg) twice daily before meals with warm water
drug_interactions: Thyroid hormones — Guggulu may enhance thyroid activity; monitor in hypothyroid patients on levothyroxine
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Sutra Sthana 23 (Santarpaniya); Sharangdhara Samhita

name: Trayodashanga Guggulu
dosage: 1–2 tablets (500mg) twice daily with warm water or castor oil
classical_text_reference: Sharangdhara Samhita, Madhyama Khanda 7; Ashtanga Hridayam Chi.21

name: Mahayogaraja Guggulu
dosage: 1–2 tablets (500mg) twice daily with warm water — only under clinical supervision
drug_interactions: Heavy metals in formulation — avoid concurrent nephrotoxic drugs
classical_text_reference: Sharangdhara Samhita, Madhyama Khanda 7; Ashtanga Hridayam Chi.21

name: Chandrakala Rasa
dosage: 125–250mg twice daily with honey or rose water — only under clinical supervision
drug_interactions: Avoid concurrent use with nephrotoxic drugs due to mercury content
afi_reference: API Vol. I — Chandrakala Rasa (Rasa Shastra)
classical_text_reference: Rasa Tarangini 18/45; Yogaratnakara (Jwara Chikitsa)
classical_action: Pittashamana, Jwaraghna, Raktastambhana — must be from authenticated APM

name: Sutashekara Rasa
dosage: 125–250mg twice daily with honey or water — only under clinical supervision
drug_interactions: Nephrotoxic drugs — avoid concurrent use; mercury risk
afi_reference: API Vol. I — Sutashekara Rasa (Rasa Shastra)
classical_text_reference: Rasa Tarangini 12/28; Sharangdhara Samhita
classical_action: Pittashamana, Deepana, Yakritottejaka — only authentic APM source

name: Kamadugdha Rasa
dosage: 125–250mg twice daily with honey, cream, or rose water
classical_text_reference: Rasa Tarangini 12; Bhaishajya Ratnavali (Pittaroga)

name: Laghu Sootashekara Rasa
dosage: 125–250mg with honey or water twice daily — clinical supervision only
classical_text_reference: Rasa Tarangini 12/36; Bhaishajya Ratnavali (Shiroroga Chikitsa)

name: Eladi Vati
dosage: 1–2 tablets (125mg each) dissolved or chewed as needed
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 10 (Chardi/Vomiting); Sharangdhara Samhita

name: Khadiradi Vati
dosage: 1–2 tablets sucked/dissolved in mouth 2–3 times daily after meals
anupana: Dissolve in mouth — oral use only
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Uttara Sthana 22 (Mukharoga); Charaka Samhita Chi.26
classical_action: Kusthaghna, Kriminashaka, Chedana — oral cavity use

name: Karpuradi Taila
dosage: Apply topically 2–3 times daily; inhale steam for respiratory use
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Sutra 22 (Bahya Sneha); Sharangdhara Samhita
classical_action: Vedanasthapana, Kasahara, Vatahara — external Bahya Chikitsa

name: Chandanadi Taila
ingredients: Coconut oil or sesame oil base
dosage: Apply to affected area 1–2 times daily; for headache apply to forehead and temples
dosage_pediatric: Safe for topical use in children
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Uttara Sthana 31 (Kshudra Roga); Charaka Samhita Chi.7
classical_action: Pittashamana, Varnya, Kusthaghna — external Bahya Chikitsa

name: Pinda Taila
dosage: Apply warm to affected areas 1–2 times daily; use as Abhyanga
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Sutra Sthana 22/22 (Abhyanga); CS Su.5
classical_action: Vatahara, Snehana, Brumhana — classical Abhyanga oil

name: Eladi Taila
dosage: Apply to affected skin areas 1–2 times daily after bath
dosage_pediatric: Safe for topical use in children
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Uttara 31 (Kshudra Roga); Charaka Samhita Chi.7
classical_action: Kusthaghna, Varnya, Pittashamana — external Bahya Chikitsa

name: Anu Taila
dosage: 2–3 drops in each nostril (Nasya) after warming oil, in the morning — Nasya use only
anupana: Nasya (nasal instillation) only — do not ingest
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Sutra Sthana 20 (Nasya Vidhi); Charaka Samhita Su.5
classical_action: Shirovirechana, Chedana, Nasya — classical Pratimarsha Nasya oil

name: Dashamoola Taila
dosage: External: warm application for Abhyanga daily; Internal (oral): 15–30ml with warm water under guidance
anupana: Warm water (oral form); apply warm topically
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 21 (Vatavyadhi); Charaka Samhita Chi.28
classical_action: Vatahara, Brumhana, Vedanasthapana — major Vata oil

name: Vishagarbha Taila
dosage: Apply 3–5ml topically to painful area and massage gently — EXTERNAL ONLY
anupana: External use only — contains purified Visha (Aconite); NEVER ingest
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa 21 (Vatavyadhi); Sharangdhara Samhita
classical_action: Vatahara, Vedanasthapana — potent analgesic external oil

name: Abhraka Bhasma
ingredients: Abhraka (Biotite mica) — calcined minimum 100 times (Sahasraputi class preferred)
dosage: 125–250mg with honey, ghee, or mishri twice daily — only under clinical supervision
classical_text_reference: Rasa Tarangini 5/1 (Abhraka Shodhana); Rasa Ratna Samucchaya
classical_action: Rasayana, Balya, Shwasahara, Ojovardhana — must be certified 100-puti or above

name: Tamra Bhasma
ingredients: Tamra (Copper) — purified and calcined through classical shodhana process, APM-certified only
dosage: 15–30mg with honey or ghee twice daily — SPECIALIST ONLY; certified sources mandatory
drug_interactions: Nephrotoxic drugs — avoid concurrent use; copper accumulation risk
classical_text_reference: Rasa Tarangini 13 (Tamra Shodhana); Rasa Ratna Samucchaya
classical_action: Yakritottejaka, Pandughna, Pramehaghna — specialist use only; APM-certified

name: Ashwagandharishta
dosage: 15–30 mL with equal water after meals twice daily
dosage_pediatric: 5–10 mL with equal water after meals
classical_text_reference: Bhaishajya Ratnavali, Vatavyadhi Adhikara; Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana 1 (Rasayana Pada)

name: Mahatikta Ghrita
dosage: 5–10 g on empty stomach with warm water
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa Sthana 19 (Kushtha Chikitsa); Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana 7

name: Phalatrikadi Kashayam
dosage: 15–30 mL with equal warm water twice daily before meals
classical_text_reference: Sahasrayoga, Kashaya Prakarana; Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa Sthana 15 (Kamala Chikitsa)

name: Agnitundi Vati
dosage: 1–2 tablets (250 mg each) with warm water before meals
classical_text_reference: Bhaishajya Ratnavali, Grahani Roga Adhikara; Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana 15 (Grahani Chikitsa)

name: Rajapravartini Vati
dosage: 1–2 tablets (125 mg each) twice daily with warm water during menstrual cycle
classical_text_reference: Bhaishajya Ratnavali, Rakta Pradara Adhikara; Yogaratnakara, Stri Roga section

name: Kanakasava
dosage: 15–30 mL with equal water twice daily after meals
classical_text_reference: Bhaishajya Ratnavali, Shwasa-Kasa Adhikara; Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana 17 (Kasa Chikitsa)

name: Varunaadi Kashayam
dosage: 15–30 mL with equal warm water twice daily before meals
classical_text_reference: Sahasrayoga, Kashaya Prakarana; Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa Sthana 11 (Mutraghata Chikitsa)

name: Rohitaka Churna
dosage: 3–5 g with warm water twice daily before meals
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana 13 (Gulma Chikitsa); Sushruta Samhita, Uttara Tantra

name: Satavaryadi Ghrita
dosage: 5–10 g with warm milk twice daily
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Uttara Sthana 34 (Yonivyapad Chikitsa); Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana 30 (Vajikarana Pada)

name: Shwas Kuthar Rasa
dosage: 1–2 tablets (125 mg each) twice daily with Sitopaladi Churna and honey
anupana: Honey with Sitopaladi Churna or Ginger juice
classical_text_reference: Bhaishajya Ratnavali, Shwasa-Kasa Adhikara; Rasa Tarangini, Rasa Aushadhi section

name: Panchavalkal Kashayam
dosage: 15–30 mL with equal warm water twice daily; externally as wound wash
dosage_pediatric: 5–10 mL with warm water; diluted 1:2 for wound wash
classical_text_reference: Sushruta Samhita, Sutrasthana 38 (Dravyasamgrahaniya); Ashtanga Hridayam, Sutrasthana 15

name: Guggulutiktakam Kashayam
dosage: 15–30 mL with equal warm water twice daily before meals
classical_text_reference: Sahasrayoga, Kashaya Prakarana; Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa Sthana 21 (Vatarakta Chikitsa)

name: Narasimha Rasayana
dosage: 10–20 g twice daily with warm milk
classical_text_reference: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsa Sthana 1 (Rasayana Pada); Ashtanga Hridayam, Uttara Sthana 39

name: Dhanwanthari Gulika
dosage: 1 tablet (500 mg) with water or butter-milk twice daily during fever
dosage_pediatric: Half tablet with honey or buttermilk
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa Sthana 1 (Jvara Chikitsa); Sahasrayoga, Gulika Prakarana

name: Ashtachurna
dosage: 1–3 g with warm water or buttermilk before meals
classical_text_reference: Ashtanga Hridayam, Chikitsa Sthana 10 (Atisara Chikitsa); Bhaishajya Ratnavali, Ajirna Adhikara
